prp.objective: Wire the stagehand CLI's main surface: cobra root command `Run` (the default action), all PRD §15.2 persistent global flags, precedence loading via `config.Flags` (CLI flags + STAGEHAND_* env), the default action flow (provider validation → maybeAutoStage → `stagehand.GenerateCommit` → sentinel→exit-code mapping), --version short-circuit, and TTY-aware verbose/color. Staging POLICY (maybeAutoStage) is owned here but kept in a separate, testable helper so the v2 seam (CommitStaged assumes the index is already staged) stays clean. Mode A docs: materialize the CLI flag/env reference in docs/CONFIGURATION.md + set cobra Long/Short help.
prp.context:
## Goal

**Feature Goal**: The runnable default action — `stagehand [flags]` resolves config (flag>env>git-config>file>defaults via config.Load), validates the provider exists+is-on-PATH (friendly error), runs maybeAutoStage (HasStagedChanges/AddAll per FR16–FR20), calls `stagehand.GenerateCommit`, and maps the returned sentinel error to the exact PRD §15.4 exit code (0/1/2/3), with verbose (FR50) and TTY-aware color (FR51). `--version` short-circuits via cobra's built-in Version field. The ten PRD §15.2 global flags are registered as persistent flags on rootCmd.

**Deliverable**: (a) EDIT `cmd/stagehand/main.go` — add the persistent flags, set `rootCmd.Run` (or RunE) wiring `os.Exit(runDefault(...))`, set `rootCmd.Long`; (b) CREATE `cmd/stagehand/run.go` — owns the default-action logic as PURE, testable helpers (`buildFlags`, `resolveAndCheckProvider`, `maybeAutoStage`, `mapErrorToExitCode`, `runDefault`); (c) CREATE `cmd/stagehand/run_test.go` — white-box `package main` tests for the MOCKING contract; (d) EDIT `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` — add the §15.2 CLI flag table + the §15.4 exit-code table (Mode A). DO NOT touch providers.go/config.go/main.go's `version` var or the `rootCmd` Version field.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./cmd/stagehand/`, `go vet ./cmd/stagehand/`, gofmt clean, and `go test ./...` all pass; `stagehand --version` prints `stagehand version <v>` and exits 0; bare `stagehand` (in a repo with staged changes + a detected agent) runs the default action and exits 0; nothing-staged + `--no-auto-stage` exits 2; missing/undetected provider exits 1 with the friendly message; precedence flag>env is asserted by test.

## User Persona

**Target User**: a developer who has staged changes and wants an AI-generated conventional commit message.
**Use Case**: `stagehand` (bare) in a repo with staged changes; `stagehand --dry-run`; `stagehand --provider claude`; `stagehand --no-auto-stage`.
**User Journey**: invoke → config resolved → (trust notice if repo-local provider) → (auto-stage if nothing staged & auto_stage_all) → generate → print `[sha] subject` + diff-tree → exit 0 (or 2/3/1).
**Pain Points Addressed**: no need to remember a message format; transparent verbose mode; safe exit codes for scripting.

## Why
- This IS the product's main surface (M7 milestone capstone for the default action).
- Integrates the shipped pkg/stagehand.GenerateCommit (M7.T1.S1), config.Load (M5.T3.S1), provider.Registry (M2.T3.S2), git.HasStagedChanges/AddAll (M3.T4.S2), ui exitcodes+Output (M1.T2.S1/S2).
- Preserves the v2 seam: staging POLICY lives ONLY here, never inside CommitStaged (decisions.md §1/§6).

## What

### User-visible behavior
- `stagehand` (bare, staged changes present, agent detected): generates + commits, prints `[<short-sha>] <subject>` then `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` to stdout (FR42), exits 0.
- `stagehand --version`: prints `stagehand version <v>`, exits 0 (cobra Version field).
- `stagehand --dry-run`: prints the generated message to stdout, does NOT commit, exits 0 (FR49).
- `stagehand --no-auto-stage` with nothing staged: prints a friendly message to stderr, exits 2 (FR17).
- Nothing staged + auto_stage_all (default): runs `git add -A`, prints `Nothing staged — staging all changes.` to stderr (FR18), re-checks; still clean → exit 2 (FR17).
- `stagehand --all`/`-a`: force `git add -A` even when something is already staged (FR20).
- Undetected/unknown provider: `Provider <name>: command <cmd> not found. Is the agent installed?` to stderr, exit 1.
- Generation failure (timeout/agent-error/parse-fail/dup-exhaustion): rescue block printed to stderr by CommitStaged, exit 3 (ErrRescue).
- Repo-local provider redirect: one-line §19 trust notice to stderr before generation.
- `--verbose`/`-v`/`STAGEHAND_VERBOSE=1`: the resolved command, raw agent stdout, and each retry are printed to stderr (FR50) — emitted by CommitStaged's Output.Verbosef, gated by cfg.Verbose which the CLI sets.
- Color auto-enables on a TTY stdout, disables with `--no-color`/`STAGEHAND_NO_COLOR`/`NO_COLOR` (FR51).

### Success Criteria
- [ ] All ten PRD §15.2 flags registered as persistent on rootCmd with exact names + shorthands (-a, -v).
- [ ] buildFlags produces a config.Flags{Env, Flag} where each FlagsLayer pointer is set ONLY when the source is present (env via os.LookupEnv; flag via cmd.Flags().Changed), honoring the present-but-zero rule.
- [ ] Precedence flag>env>git-config>file asserted by test (buildFlags + config.Load).
- [ ] Provider validation: exit 1 + friendly message when resolved provider is unknown OR not detected on PATH.
- [ ] Default Run calls config.Load(., "."), prints trust notice if non-empty, validates provider, maybeAutoStage, then stagehand.GenerateCommit.
- [ ] Exit-code mapping exact: nil→0; ErrNothingToCommit→2; ErrRescue→3; ErrHeadMoved/other→1. (Timeout during generation collapses to ErrRescue→3 per the shipped CommitStaged; documented. ExitTimeout(124) reserved/unused in v1.)
- [ ] The CLI does NOT re-print the FR42 success block or the DryRun message (CommitStaged owns stdout via out.Resultf).
- [ ] --version short-circuits via cobra's Version field (no manual --version flag).
- [ ] docs/CONFIGURATION.md gains the CLI flag table (§15.2) + exit-code table (§15.4); rootCmd.Long set.

## All Needed Context

### Context Completeness Check
Passes the "No Prior Knowledge" test: every dependency signature is quoted verbatim, the exit-code/timeout tension is resolved with a citation, the flag→FlagsLayer wiring rules are spelled out (present-but-zero, os.LookupEnv vs Changed), and the testable-helper decomposition mirrors the shipped signal.go `handleSignal(...) int` seam.

  pattern: pure helpers take (cfg, reg, detected) and return (name, model); RunE is thin glue.

  why: GenerateCommit(ctx, Options) (Result, error). Options{Provider,Model,SystemExtra string; DryRun bool; Timeout time.Duration}. Returns sentinels ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved. Calls config.Load(config.Flags{}, ".") ITSELF — so the CLI MUST pass resolved Provider/Model/Timeout via opts to override. Prints FR42 success block + DryRun message via out.Resultf (stdout) — CLI must NOT re-print.
  gotcha: GenerateCommit makes its OWN git.New(".") and ui.NewOutput(verbose=false,noColor=false). So the CLI's verbose/color resolution is passed via cfg (config.Load) BUT GenerateCommit ignores cfg.Verbose/cfg.NoColor when building its own Output! ⟹ The verbose/color the user sees during GenerateCommit is ALWAYS false unless... verify: GenerateCommit hardcodes ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, false, false). This means FR50 verbose during the GENERATE step is NOT observable through GenerateCommit in v1. DOCUMENT this as a known gap: verbose printing of the resolved command/raw stdout/retries is owned by CommitStaged's Output, which GenerateCommit constructs with verbose=false. To honor FR50 in v1, the CLI should call a FUTURE GenerateCommit variant OR accept that verbose currently only affects CLI-level Progressf lines. NOTE this in the help text and the success criteria (verbose flag is wired into cfg.Verbose + the CLI's own out; full FR50 agent-trace verbose is a follow-up since GenerateCommit hardcodes verbose=false).
- file: internal/config/load.go

  gotcha: NON-NIL pointer to ZERO value counts as SET (overwrites). Set pointer ONLY when source present.
- file: internal/provider/registry.go
  why: reg.Get(name)(Manifest,bool); reg.List()[]string (sorted); reg.Detect()map[string]bool (LookPath on m.Detect||m.Command). Use Detect to validate the resolved provider is installed.
- file: internal/git/stage.go
  why: HasStagedChanges()(bool,error) — exit0→false(clean)/exit1→true(staged); AddAll() error. PRIMITIVES ONLY; staging POLICY is THIS task's job.
- file: internal/ui/exitcode.go
  why: ExitSuccess=0, ExitError=1, ExitNothingToCommit=2, ExitRescue=3, ExitTimeout=124. Use these constants — NEVER hardcode ints.
- file: internal/ui/output.go
  why: NewOutput(stdout,stderr,verbose,noColor); Progressf→stderr ALWAYS (trust notice, auto-stage notice, errors); Resultf→stdout; Verbosef→stderr gated. Color auto-disables when stdout is not a TTY.

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models and structure
```go
package main
import (
    "context"
    "errors"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "strings"
    "time"
    "github.com/spf13/cobra"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/config"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/git"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/provider"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/ui"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/pkg/stagehand"
)
func buildFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) config.Flags {  }
func resolveAndCheckProvider(cfg config.Config, reg *provider.Registry) (string, int, string) {  }
func maybeAutoStagePolicy(g *git.Git, out *ui.Output, cfg config.Config, allFlag, noAutoStage bool) int {  }
func mapErrorToExitCode(err error) int {
    if err == nil { return ui.ExitSuccess }
    if errors.Is(err, stagehand.ErrNothingToCommit) { return ui.ExitNothingToCommit }
    if errors.Is(err, stagehand.ErrRescue) { return ui.ExitRescue }
    return ui.ExitError 
}
func runDefault(cmd *cobra.Command) int {  }

```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details
```go
func main() {
    rootCmd.Run = func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) { os.Exit(runDefault(cmd)) }
    if err := rootCmd.Execute(); err != nil { os.Exit(ui.ExitError) }
}
func runDefault(cmd *cobra.Command) int {
    flags := buildFlags(cmd)
    cfg, reg, notice, err := config.Load(flags, ".")
    out := ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, cfg.Verbose, cfg.NoColor)
    if err != nil { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err); return ui.ExitError }
    if notice != "" { out.Progressf("%s\n", notice) }   
    name, code, msg := resolveAndCheckProvider(cfg, reg)
    if code != 0 { out.Progressf("%s\n", msg); return code }
    g, err := git.New(".")
    if err != nil { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err); return ui.ExitError }
    allFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("all")
    noAutoStage, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-auto-stage")
    if code := maybeAutoStagePolicy(g, out, cfg, allFlag, noAutoStage); code != 0 { return code }
    dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
    opts := stagehand.Options{Provider: cfg.Provider, Model: cfg.Model, Timeout: cfg.Timeout, DryRun: dryRun}
    _, err = stagehand.GenerateCommit(context.Background(), opts)
    return mapErrorToExitCode(err)   
}
func buildFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) config.Flags {
    f := config.Flags{}
    if v, ok := os.LookupEnv("STAGEHAND_PROVIDER"); ok && v != "" { f.Env.Provider = &v }
    if cmd.Flags().Changed("provider") { v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider"); f.Flag.Provider = &v }
    return f
}

```

## Final Validation Checklist
### Technical
- [ ] All 4 validation levels pass.
- [ ] run.go uses plain `package main`; main.go keeps the // Package doc + version var + Version field.
- [ ] providers.go / config.go UNCHANGED (diff empty for those files).
- [ ] No testify; stdlib + cobra + internal/* + pkg/stagehand only.
- [ ] gofmt clean (gofmt -l empty).

### Feature
- [ ] All ten PRD §15.2 persistent flags registered with exact names + shorthands.
- [ ] buildFlags honors present-but-zero (flag via Changed; env via LookupEnv).
- [ ] Precedence flag>env asserted by test.
- [ ] Provider missing → friendly msg + exit 1.
- [ ] Exit-code mapping exact (nil→0, ErrNothingToCommit→2, ErrRescue→3, other→1).
- [ ] --version short-circuits via Version field.
- [ ] --dry-run wired into opts.DryRun; nothing-staged+--no-auto-stage → exit 2.
- [ ] CLI writes notices to stderr ONLY (FR51 stdout-clean); does NOT re-print FR42.

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/CONFIGURATION.md has the CLI flag table (§15.2) + exit-code table (§15.4).
- [ ] rootCmd.Long set to a helpful multi-line synopsis.
- [ ] Mode A DOCS impact materialized (not deferred to Mode B).

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- ❌ Don't re-implement the generate pipeline — call stagehand.GenerateCommit.
- ❌ Don't re-print the FR42 success block / DryRun message (CommitStaged owns stdout).
- ❌ Don't hardcode exit ints — use ui.Exit* constants.
- ❌ Don't use RunE for codes 2/3 (cobra maps err→1) — use Run + os.Exit(runDefault()).
- ❌ Don't add a manual --version flag (cobra Version field handles it).
- ❌ Don't set a FlagsLayer pointer for an absent env var (use LookupEnv, not Getenv).
- ❌ Don't touch providers.go/config.go/main.go's version var or siblings' init().
- ❌ Don't implement staging policy inside CommitStaged (v2 seam — keep it in run.go).
- ❌ Don't add testify or external deps.
- ❌ Don't drop the timeout→rescue→3 documentation (PRD §15.4 lists 124 but the shipped architecture collapses timeout into ErrRescue→3; document it, don't silently special-case 124).
implementationSteps: CREATE cmd/stagehand/run.go (plain `package main`): pure, os.Exit-free helpers — buildFlags(cmd) config.Flags (env via os.LookupEnv, flag via cmd.Flags().Changed, honoring present-but-zero; parse STAGEHAND_TIMEOUT with time.ParseDuration), resolveAndCheckProvider(cfg, reg) (name, exitCode, msg) implementing cfg.Provider→first-detected + unknown/!detected→friendly 'Provider X: command Y not found. Is the agent installed?', maybeAutoStagePolicy(g, out, cfg, allFlag, noAutoStage) int implementing FR16–FR20 (return 2 to short-circuit, 0 to proceed), mapErrorToExitCode(err) int (nil→0, ErrNothingToCommit→2, ErrRescue→3, other→1), and runDefault(cmd) int per the research-note §5 flow (Load→trust notice→provider check→maybeAutoStage→GenerateCommit→map). Define a minimal `stager` interface {HasStagedChanges() (bool,error); AddAll() error} so *git.Git + a test stub both satisfy it.

EDIT cmd/stagehand/main.go: in an init() (or package-var block) register the ten PRD §15.2 persistent flags on rootCmd.PersistentFlags() with exact names+shorthands (--provider,--model,--config,--timeout Duration,--all/-a,--no-auto-stage,--dry-run,--verbose/-v,--no-color; NOT --version/--help — cobra built-ins); set rootCmd.Run = func(cmd,*[]string){ os.Exit(runDefault(cmd)) }; set rootCmd.Long to a multi-line synopsis (PRD §15.1); change main()'s error exit to os.Exit(ui.ExitError). PRESERVE var version, Version: version field, and the siblings' init() registrations.

CREATE cmd/stagehand/run_test.go (white-box package main, stdlib + cobra + internal/* + pkg/stagehand, NO testify): TestBuildFlags_FlagOverEnv, TestBuildFlags_PresentButZero, TestBuildFlags_EnvAbsent, TestResolveAndCheckProvider_MissingFriendlyMsg (fabricated not-on-PATH provider → exitError + 'command ... not found' + 'Is the agent installed?'), TestResolveAndCheckProvider_FirstDetected, TestMapErrorToExitCode (table over sentinels), TestMaybeAutoStage_NothingStagedNoAutoStageExit2 (stub stager), TestVersionShortCircuit (temp cobra cmd + Version + --version → stdout 'stagehand version'), TestDryRunWiredIntoOpts (pure buildOptions helper → opts.DryRun==true).

EDIT docs/CONFIGURATION.md (Mode A): add a 'CLI flags' section with the PRD §15.2 table (Flag, Env, Git config, Default, Description) for all ten flags, add the PRD §15.4 exit-code table (0/1/2/3/124) with the v1 timeout→rescue→3 note, and replace the existing P1.M7.T2.S1 placeholder cross-reference with a live link to the new section.

RUN the validation gates in order (go build ./cmd/stagehand/, go vet ./cmd/stagehand/, go test ./cmd/stagehand/ -v, go test ./...) and fix every failure before declaring done; verify providers.go and config.go are UNCHANGED (git diff --exit-code for those files).

successCriteria.description: go build ./cmd/stagehand/ succeeds with no errors

go test ./cmd/stagehand/ -v passes (buildFlags precedence present-but-zero, resolveAndCheckProvider friendly message + first-detected, mapErrorToExitCode table, maybeAutoStage nothing-staged+--no-auto-stage→2, --version short-circuit, --dry-run wired)
go test ./... passes (no regression in providers/config/git/generate/stagehand packages)
All ten PRD §15.2 persistent flags registered with exact names + shorthands (-a, -v); --version handled by cobra Version field (no manual flag)
Exit-code mapping exact: nil→0, ErrNothingToCommit→2, ErrRescue→3, ErrHeadMoved/other→1 (ui.Exit* constants used, no hardcoded ints)
Provider validation: unknown OR not-on-PATH resolved provider → friendly 'Provider X: command Y not found. Is the agent installed?' to stderr + exit 1
buildFlags honors present-but-zero rule (env via os.LookupEnv presence; flag via cmd.Flags().Changed) and precedence flag>env>git-config>file is asserted by test
CLI writes trust notice + auto-stage notice + errors to stderr ONLY (FR51 stdout-clean); does NOT re-print the FR42 success block or DryRun message (CommitStaged owns stdout)
providers.go and config.go are UNCHANGED (git diff --exit-code shows no edits); the siblings' init() self-registrations still work
Mode A docs materialized: docs/CONFIGURATION.md has the PRD §15.2 CLI flag table + §15.4 exit-code table; rootCmd.Long set

references: PRD.md §15.1 (synopsis), §15.2 (global flags), §15.4 (exit codes), §19 (trust notice), FR16–FR20, FR42, FR49–FR51
cmd/stagehand/main.go (EDIT target — rootCmd, version var, Version field)
cmd/stagehand/providers.go (house pattern + resolveDefault helper to reuse)
pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go (GenerateCommit/Options/Result/sentinels — calls config.Load(config.Flags{},".") itself; hardcodes ui.NewOutput(verbose=false))
docs/CONFIGURATION.md (Mode A doc target — CLI flag + exit-code tables)
